rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Respondent Tandc Properties Llcs Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs In The Post Arbitration Judicial Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 15, 2023 line 1. T & C PROPERTIES LLC'S SECOND AMENDED MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IN THE POST ARBITRATION JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS is DENIED. T&C Properties is not the prevailing party in the litigation phase of the dispute. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 15, 2023 line 1. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS is DENIED without prejudice in part. Hearing Required in part. The court determines that St. James is the prevailing party in the litigation phase of this matter (action filed on November 19, 2020) and that the arbitrator had already determined that St. James is the prevailing party in arbitration. Motion for fees incurred in the litigation phase of the dispute is DENIED without prejudice. On August 9, 2023 St. James was ordered to (1) file a supplemental brief outlining the total amount of fees incurred in the litigation phase and (2) provide a copy of the previously filed declarations with the portions of the documents evidencing the amounts claimed in the litigation phase (e.g. bills, invoices, summaries) tabbed and highlighted. St. James did neither. No supplemental declaration on file as of August 14, 2023. No highlighted and tabbed (directing the Court to the fees incurred in the litigation phase only) copies of the previously filed evidence received by the Court as of August 14, 2023. Hearing Required to address the fees incurred in arbitration. The Court is inclined to send the issue of the amounts of the fees awarded, if any, in the arbitration phase back to arbitration. The Final Award stops just short of calculating the amount after determining the prevailing party and its entitlement to the fees and costs. However, if the parties stipulate to the Court's authority to determine the issue of amount of fees, if any, the Court will consider making a ruling on the merits to avoid any prejudice to the parties caused by further delay in final resolution of this dispute submitted to arbitration in 2019. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1370 NATOMA LLC ET AL VS. LUCIA MUNOZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 15, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANTS LUCIA MUNOZ, EDDY VARGAS, AND CARLA MUNOZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT Hearing will be held at 9:00 a.m. on August 15, 2023 in department 501. Defendant's Motion to Strike the Fourth Amended Complaint is DENIED, 5 days to answer. Whether the "owner" executed the Memorandum is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. The Memorandum is signed by "Michael A. Campesino" and the notary certification is for "Michael E. Campesino." The Court is not persuaded that this discrepancy renders the Memorandum defective as a matter of law. =(501/DAF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AERC 8TH & HARRISON LLC VS. MARCELO CURY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 15, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT MARCELO CURY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS Hearing will be held at 9:00 a.m. on August 15, 2023 in department 501. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is DENIED. The motion does not identify any purported service that the moving party is seeking to be quashed. There is no evidence presented in support of the motion or on the record that Plaintiff attempted any service, proper or otherwise. In the absence of any identified service, the Court is not able to determine that service was improper and quash it. =(501/DAF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EMILY PERDONCIN VS. MICHELLE ZIMMERMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 15, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANTS MICHELLE ZIMMERMAN AND ANTHONY HERNANDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS Hearing will be held at 9:00 a.m. on August 15, 2023 in department 501. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is DENIED. The registered process server is entitled to a presumption per Evidence Code 647 regarding service and the moving party fails to rebut the presumption. =(501/DAF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
